non_blocking_notes: scripts/apply-corescope-overlay.mjs defaults to /app/corescope/public, which is Docker-oriented; Docker passes explicit paths and local verification also passes explicit paths.
Root-path WebSocket upgrades are routed to CoreScope to match its client behavior; revisit if the Next app later introduces production WebSockets at /.
verification: npm run corescope:apply-overlay -- <tmp CoreScope public copy> corescope-overlay
Docker smoke: /map returns CoreScope HTML with denvermc-default-route.js overlay
Docker smoke: /denvermc-default-route.js?v=denvermc returns overlay script containing #/live
Docker smoke: /api/config/map returns CoreScope map defaults
Docker smoke: /api/packets?limit=1 and /api/packets/timestamps?since=2020-01-01 route to CoreScope without nginx slash redirects
Docker smoke: /api/map/runtime remains served by Next compatibility API
review_summary: Step 4 routes /map to same-origin CoreScope, preserves Next compatibility APIs, injects a local non-submodule overlay to default /map to #/live, and replaces the non-Docker fallback copy without correctness blockers.
